# Implementation Plan: PowerOffScreensaver

**Branch**: `001-power-off-screensaver` | **Date**: 2026-06-05 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-power-off-screensaver/spec.md`

**Note**: This file was generated by `/speckit-plan`.

---

## Summary

Build a Windows screensaver (`.scr` / `.exe`) in C# / .NET 8 / WinForms that covers every connected monitor with a fullscreen black window, sends `WM_SYSCOMMAND SC_MONITORPOWER` to power off displays, and calls `LockWorkStation()` on any mouse/keyboard input. All Win32 P/Invoke lives in `src/Services/`. DDC/CI is behind an interface stub and ships disabled by default. No admin elevation, no registry writes, no network access.

---

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: C# 12 / .NET 8

**Primary Dependencies**: WinForms (`net8.0-windows`), `System.Text.Json` (built-in), xUnit (tests only)

**Storage**: `%AppData%\PowerOffScreensaver\settings.json` — UTF-8 JSON; no database; no registry

**Testing**: xUnit 2.x — unit tests for arg parser, settings, dead-zone logic, and host exit guard; manual for UI/multi-monitor

**Target Platform**: Windows 10/11 x64 — single self-contained `.exe`

**Project Type**: Desktop application (screensaver)

**Performance Goals**: Black windows visible within 500 ms of `/s` launch; exit within 100 ms of first input event

**Constraints**: No admin elevation for `/s` path; binary ≤ 10 MB published; `dotnet build` 0 errors 0 warnings

**Scale/Scope**: Single-user local app; N monitors (N ≥ 1); no concurrency beyond WinForms message loop

---

## Constitution Check

*All gates pass before implementation begins.*

- [x] **I. Multi-Monitor** — `Screen.AllScreens` loop creates one `BlackoutForm` per display; no single-monitor shortcut
- [x] **II. Screensaver Protocol** — `Program.cs` parses `/s`, `/c`, `/p`; binary compiles to `.exe` and works when renamed `.scr`
- [x] **III. Win32 Isolation** — all `DllImport` / P/Invoke in `src/Services/`; `BlackoutForm` and `Program.cs` contain zero P/Invoke
- [x] **IV. Minimal Footprint** — WinForms only; self-contained publish; no admin for `/s`; no power-plan changes
- [x] **V. Safety** — keyboard hook is read-only exit trigger; no logging of keys/mouse coords; `LockWorkStation()` called on exit; no network
- [x] **VI. Russian Language** — spec, plan, tasks, analysis in Russian; code identifiers and commit messages in English

---

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-power-off-screensaver/
├── plan.md              ← this file
├── spec.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── cli-arguments.md
│   └── service-interfaces.md
└── tasks.md             ← generated by /speckit-tasks (not yet created)
```

### Source Code

```text
src/
└── PowerOffScreensaver/
    ├── PowerOffScreensaver.csproj
    ├── Program.cs                         # arg parse → route to host or settings
    ├── ScreensaverArgs.cs                 # ScreensaverArgs record + LaunchMode enum
    ├── Host/
    │   └── ScreensaverHost.cs             # ApplicationContext subclass; lifecycle mgr
    ├── Forms/
    │   ├── BlackoutForm.cs                # borderless fullscreen black window
    │   └── SettingsForm.cs                # /c settings dialog
    └── Services/
        ├── IMonitorPowerService.cs
        ├── MonitorPowerService.cs         # SendMessage WM_SYSCOMMAND
        ├── IWorkstationLockService.cs
        ├── WorkstationLockService.cs      # LockWorkStation()
        ├── IDdcCiService.cs
        ├── NullDdcCiService.cs            # no-op stub (default)
        ├── ISettingsService.cs
        └── SettingsService.cs             # JSON load/save to %AppData%

tests/
└── PowerOffScreensaver.Tests/
    ├── PowerOffScreensaver.Tests.csproj
    ├── ScreensaverArgsParserTests.cs
    ├── AppSettingsTests.cs
    ├── DeadZoneTests.cs
    └── ScreensaverHostExitOnceTests.cs
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-project layout. No web backend, no mobile, no monorepo tooling. `Services/` enforces the Win32 isolation boundary mandated by Principle III.

---

## Phase 0 — Research

*Complete. See [research.md](research.md).*

Key decisions resolved:

| Topic | Decision |
|---|---|
| Monitor enumeration | `Screen.AllScreens` for MVP |
| Power-off signal | `SendMessage(HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SYSCOMMAND, SC_MONITORPOWER, 2)` |
| Workstation lock | `LockWorkStation()` from user32.dll |
| Fullscreen form | Borderless + explicit `Bounds = screen.Bounds` (avoids taskbar gap) |
| Mouse dead zone | Fixed 5 px Euclidean; not configurable in MVP |
| Settings storage | `System.Text.Json` → `%AppData%\PowerOffScreensaver\settings.json` |
| DDC/CI | `NullDdcCiService` stub; real implementation deferred |
| Host lifecycle | Custom `ApplicationContext`; Interlocked flag prevents double-exit |

---

## Implementation Phases (for `/speckit-tasks`)

### Phase A — MVP Core (`/s` only)

Deliverable: `PowerOffScreensaver.exe /s` works end-to-end on 2+ monitors.

1. Create solution + project (`PowerOffScreensaver.csproj`, `net8.0-windows`, WinForms)
2. `ScreensaverArgs.cs` — record + parser (all argument variants; unit tested)
3. `AppSettings.cs` — record with defaults
4. `SettingsService.cs` + `ISettingsService` — JSON load/save + default fallback
5. `MonitorPowerService.cs` + `IMonitorPowerService` — `SendMessage` P/Invoke
6. `WorkstationLockService.cs` + `IWorkstationLockService` — `LockWorkStation()` P/Invoke
7. `NullDdcCiService.cs` + `IDdcCiService` — no-op stub
8. `BlackoutForm.cs` — fullscreen black window, mouse dead zone, `ExitRequested` event
9. `ScreensaverHost.cs` — `ApplicationContext`; creates forms; subscribes exit; calls lock
10. `Program.cs` — parses args; routes to `ScreensaverHost`; handles `/p` no-op
11. Unit tests (xUnit): arg parser, settings round-trip, dead-zone logic, exit-once guard
12. Manual test: `dotnet run -- /s` on multi-monitor setup

### Phase B — Settings Form (`/c`)

Deliverable: `PowerOffScreensaver.exe /c` opens working settings dialog.

1. `SettingsForm.cs` — checkboxes + delay input + Test button
2. Wire `Program.cs` `/c` path → `SettingsForm`
3. Persistence verification test

### Phase C — DDC/CI (optional, post-MVP)

Deliverable: Real `DdcCiService` implementation behind the existing interface.

1. Research `WindowsDisplayAPI` NuGet vs direct IOCTL
2. Implement `DdcCiService.cs` (VCP 0xD6)
3. Gate behind `AppSettings.DdcCiEnabled`
4. Manual test on DDC/CI-capable monitor

### Phase D — Documentation & Distribution

1. `README.md` — build, install, verify, troubleshooting
2. Publish script (`publish.ps1`)
3. Verify `.scr` rename workflow in Windows Screen Saver Settings
